title: DIR General Prevailing Wage Determinations Menu 2026-1

indexed_extract.summary: 2026-1 general prevailing wage journeyman determinations index by county, including Alameda and Contra Costa with predetermined increase references.
key_points: DIR publishes the current county menu for 2026-1 determinations.
This is the canonical navigation anchor for county/craft wage seeding.
Should drive cycle-aware seed ingestion rather than union-website-first logic.

notes: Use as the canonical menu/root for Bay Area county wage crawl.

indexed_extract.summary: DIR states public works contracts valued at $30,000 or more carry an apprenticeship obligation, with DAS 140 and DAS 142 workflow and 1 apprentice hour per 5 journeyman hours.
key_points: Public works contracts valued at $30,000+ require apprentices unless craft is not apprenticeable.
DAS 140 notice and DAS 142 dispatch request responsibilities are explicit.
Minimum ratio is one apprentice hour per five straight-time journeyman hours.

title: DIR Division of Apprenticeship Standards - Minimum Ratios

indexed_extract.summary: DIR explains the 1:5 minimum ratio and notes craft/program-specific exemptions, including Northern California Operating Engineers 1:4.
key_points: Minimum ratio is 5 journeymen hours to 1 apprentice hour per craft, straight time only.
Specific programs may have different maximum/minimum ratios.
Northern California Operating Engineers example uses 1 apprentice for each 4 journeymen.

notes: Important for modeling craft-specific ratio overrides separately from default rule.

title: Caltrans Equipment Rental Rates and Labor Surcharge

indexed_extract.summary: Caltrans publishes labor surcharge and equipment rental rate books in PDF and accessible CSV, including 04/01/2025 through 03/31/2026.
key_points: Accessible CSV files exist and should be treated as canonical public-works equipment seed inputs.
The current cycle listed is 04/01/2025 through 03/31/2026.
Model as effective-dated equipment rate books with ingestion manifests.

title: SF DBI Fees Resource

indexed_extract.summary: SF DBI publishes permit, plan review, and subject-specific fee tables, including new construction permit fees based on valuation.
key_points: DBI fee resource is the canonical city page for permit/inspection fee references.
Fee tables are separated by subject and should be mirrored into fee schedule seed packs.
Supports placeholder generation tied to valuation and permit category.

notes: Canonical SF DBI fee anchor; underlying tables still need mirroring by subject.

title: SF Public Works General Excavation Permit

indexed_extract.summary: General Excavation permits require application, drawings, utility authorization, administrative and inspection fees, possible street space/no-parking fees, insurance, business registration, and a $25,000 street excavation bond.
key_points: Permit fee generally combines administrative + inspection fees + supplemental fees.
Street Space and no-parking sign fees may also apply.
A $25,000 bond is required and should be modeled as a permit-path requirement rather than a fee line item.

notes: Important SF right-of-way permit logic source.

title: 2025-2026 San Francisco Public Works Permit Fee Schedule

indexed_extract.summary: Public Works permit fee schedule lists permit fee amounts and basis-of-fee entries, including street space and consultation/pre-application fees.
key_points: Use as fee evidence source for right-of-way and excavation-related placeholders.
Needs PDF-to-markdown or table extraction for structured fee seeding.
Should be mirrored as a parsed fee schedule source document.

notes: Download and table-extract in the next mirror pass.

title: Construction General Permit

indexed_extract.summary: Projects disturbing one acre or more, or less than one acre as part of a larger common plan totaling one acre or more, require CGP coverage.
key_points: This is a trigger rule, not a direct fee table.
Use for permit-path warnings, checklist items, and proposal assumptions.
Should generate not-applicable / warning / blocking outputs depending on site-disturbance inputs.

notes: Model as threshold logic with SMARTS workflow hints.

title: BAAQMD Regulation 11 Rule 2

indexed_extract.summary: Demolition/renovation asbestos notification generally requires 10 working days; certain small residential cases may allow 72-hour start with fees.
key_points: This is a strong Bay Area trigger for demolition/renovation work packages.
Should create permit-path checklist notes and possible blocking/warning logic depending on scope.
Needs mirrored PDF + extracted structured trigger notes.

notes: Regional compliance source for demo/renovation projects.

title: White Cap Site Search Pricing Snapshot

indexed_extract.summary: White Cap displays public item pricing but explicitly states item pricing and delivery options may vary based on location.
key_points: Use as vendor/location-scoped evidence, not canonical price truth.
Supports price_confidence and location_scope modeling.
Good for public list-price observations and curated material seeds.

notes: Vendor evidence source; do not promote directly to canonical rate books without review.
